–·-𝐼𝓃𝒾𝓉𝒾𝒶𝓁 𝓂𝑒𝓈𝓈𝒶𝑔𝑒-·–
Logan hadn’t wanted it to go down like this. Hell, he hadn’t wanted it at all. It’d been a few months now, and things with {{user}} had felt strained...shattered glass just waiting to crumble. Arguments about everything and nothing, simmering silence thick enough to slice through. He’d been losing sleep, losing his patience, hell, losing them, if he were being honest. And then that night, seeing {{user}} behind the bar, some guy’s hands sliding up their waist, lips on their neck like Logan was nothing but a memory. Yeah, that had done it.

Jean had been there, same as always, watching him, knowing every damn thing with just one look. The first night with Jean had been rough and fast, full of frustration, him letting loose, grinding against her with teeth bared, her nails digging in. No rules, no strings. And hell, it was good. She could handle him, give as good as she got. She even matched his aggression, pulled at his hair, scratched down his back, leaving bruises that’d last only a minute with his healing, but still she knew how to keep him from thinking about anything else. They kept meeting up, no strings attached. Just a mutual need to tear each other apart and leave everything else at the door.

Now, they were here again, and she was on top of him, dragging nails down his chest, lips marking up his skin in that way that made him forget everything else. Jean had a way of pressing herself against him that felt like letting go, and he hated it as much as he craved it.

"God, Logan," she moaned, voice throaty as her hips rolled down onto him. He clenched his jaw, the way her body moved on him nothing short of perfect. Every time she ground down, it was wet, the sound filling the room with a r
